name: Saltcorn
tagline: Build web and mobile database apps without code
metaDescription: Saltcorn is an open-source no-code builder for web and mobile database apps. Define data types, views, and actions, then self-host on your own server.
overview: Saltcorn is an open-source no-code builder for database applications. You define your data tables, then assemble pages from configurable views, datatypes, layouts, and actions to produce working web and mobile apps without writing application code.

It is aimed at people who want to build internal tools, admin panels, or small business apps but don't want to start from a blank backend. It runs on Node.js with PostgreSQL (or SQLite for quick local testing), and the whole platform is extensible through plugins.

Within the low-code / no-code category, Saltcorn focuses on self-hosting and data ownership. You can try a hosted multitenant instance, but the same codebase installs on your own virtual server, so you keep control of the application and the data it stores.
features: No-code app builder: compose apps from views, datatypes, layouts, and actions instead of hand-written code
Builds both web and mobile database applications from the same definitions
Self-hostable or available as a hosted multitenant instance at saltcorn.com
Works with PostgreSQL for production and SQLite for quick local testing
Extensible plugin system, plus a drag-and-drop layout editor (craft.js) and Blockly-based logic
Multiple install paths: npm CLI, one-line Linux installer, and Docker Compose

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to try Saltcorn locally is the desktop install using the CLI with a SQLite database. You need Node.js 20+ and npm.

steps.heading: Install the Saltcorn CLI
steps.body: Set a local npm prefix and install the CLI package globally.
steps.code:
npm config set prefix ~/.local
npm install -g @saltcorn/cli

steps.heading: Initialize a SQLite database and start the server
steps.body: Point Saltcorn at a SQLite file, reset the schema, then start serving.

steps.heading: Open the app
steps.body: Visit http://localhost:3000/ in your browser to start building. To run it again later, re-run the export line followed by saltcorn serve.

steps.heading: Alternative: run with Docker Compose
steps.body: For quick local testing you can use the bundled example compose file instead of the CLI.

useCases: Build internal tools and admin panels backed by a PostgreSQL database without writing backend code
Create small business or web/mobile database apps by configuring views and actions
Stand up a self-hosted, data-owned alternative to hosted no-code platforms on a small VPS
Prototype a data-driven app quickly using the free hosted multitenant instance before self-hosting
